§6Cadmium§f

§aVersatile element used for corrosion-resistant alloys, PVC production, and even rubber!§f

§bCadmium§f (Cd, atomic number 48) is a material needed to make cadmium steel, which serves as a substitute for technetium steel in nearly all recipes.

§6Production§f:
§b• Mustard Willow Leaves§f → acidize §e10 leaves§f with §e250 mB High-Performance Solvent§f in §bOre Acidizer§f = §e1 Cadmium Powder§f
§b• Smelt powder§f to get Cadmium Ingot

§6Uses§f:
§b• Cadmium Steel§f — alloy with steel ingot in Crucible (1 Cd nugget + steel ingot → 1 Cd steel ingot), or 8 Cd nuggets + 1 steel ingot in Crucible
§b• PVC Production§f — Chemical Plant: §e1 Cadmium Powder§f + §e250 mB Unsaturated Hydrocarbons§f + §e250 mB Chlorine Gas§f = §e2 PVC Bars§f (5s, 100 kHE/t)
§b• Rubber Production§f — Ore Acidizer: §e1 Cadmium Powder§f + §e4,000 mB Fish Oil§f = §e16 Rubber Bars§f
§b• Orange Dye substitute§f

§eTip§f: Cadmium has §edual processing paths§f. Use the Ore Acidizer for rubber or the Chemical Plant for PVC depending on what you need!
